metadata.title: Incorrect Quote Generator Free & No Sign Up | Story Generator
metadata.description: Generate funny, intentionally wrong quote exchanges for fandom characters, OCs, and roleplay groups. Fast, free, short-form, and easy to share.
metadata.keywords: incorrect quote generator, fandom quote generator, funny dialogue generator, character quote generator, fanfic prompt tool, incorrect quotes

ui.title: Incorrect Quote Generator
ui.subtitle: Generate short, intentionally wrong quote exchanges with up to six characters, or let the prompt imply the cast.
ui.prompt_placeholder: Describe the setup, misunderstanding, or joke you want the quote to twist.
ui.empty_output: Your generated quote will appear here.

toasts.creative_limit_reached: Today's free Creative uses are used up. Log in to get more credits.

random_prompts.prompt: The team is trying to explain why the kitchen fire definitely counts as a tactical success.
Two rivals are forced to co-host a school announcement and neither agrees on the script.
The healer has been banned from motivational speeches after one very specific incident.
A found-family crew is trying to look normal in public for exactly thirty seconds.

feature1.name: feature_intro
feature1.label: Overview
feature1.title: What This Incorrect Quote Generator Is Good At
feature1.description:
This tool is built for the moment when you want a fast, funny exchange instead of a full fanfic chapter. It takes a simple setup, your character names, and a few safety constraints, then returns a short speaker-labelled quote that feels easy to post, remix, or use as a writing warmup.

The focus is speed and shareability. Instead of overbuilding lore, pairings, or long scene structure, it aims for a clean wrong-context joke that fans, roleplayers, and OC creators can iterate on quickly. Treat each run as a prompt to riff on, not a finished scene.

items.title: Short-form by default
items.description: Generates compact quote exchanges instead of long prose, which matches how people actually use incorrect quote tools — short, postable, easy to remix into something larger.

items.title: Works for fandoms and OCs
items.description: Use canon characters, original characters, tabletop parties, or friend groups without needing a preset universe.

items.title: Safe enough for broad sharing
items.description: Quick toggles help avoid romance, shipping cues, or explicit language when you need cleaner outputs.

feature2.name: feature_benefits
feature2.label: Benefits
feature2.title: Why People Reach for Short Quotes Before Full Scenes
feature2.description: The main job is to reduce friction between a funny idea and a usable quote. When a joke lives or dies on the first line, this incorrect quote generator beats opening a blank doc and waiting for a full scene to come together.

items.title: Fast joke exploration
items.description: Try a premise, regenerate, and compare variations without waiting for a full scene to develop.

items.title: Better character chemistry testing
items.description: See how a group dynamic lands in a few lines before investing in a larger story or roleplay thread.

items.title: Easy handoff into longer writing
items.description: If a quote works, send it straight into AI Write and turn the joke into a longer draft.

feature3.name: use_cases
feature3.label: Use Cases
feature3.title: Best Uses: Fandom, OC, and Roleplay Banter
feature3.description: Best for lightweight creative moments where speed matters more than deep configuration. Drop a setup into the incorrect quote generator, pick a tone, and let the tool handle the dialogue shape.

items.title: Fandom creators
items.description: Spin up quick incorrect quotes for ships, gen dynamics, found family groups, or cast-wide chaos.

items.title: OC and roleplay writers
items.description: Stress-test personalities and banter before writing longer scenes or public roleplay intros.

items.title: Social posting and prompts
items.description: Generate compact, readable exchanges that are easy to post in communities, chats, or prompt threads.

how_to_use.name: how_to_use
how_to_use.label: HOW TO USE
how_to_use.title: How to Use the Incorrect Quote Generator in 3 Steps
how_to_use.description: From blank prompt to postable quote in under a minute — no signup, no setup.

items.title: Describe the setup
items.description: Type the situation, misunderstanding, or joke you want the characters to fumble. Add two to six character names, or leave it blank and let the tool fill in generic speakers.

items.title: Pick tone, length, and safety
items.description: Choose a tone (absurd, dry, sarcastic), a length (one-liner to extended exchange), and toggle clean-output safety options for public-friendly posts.

items.title: Generate, copy, or expand
items.description: Hit generate, copy the quote for sharing, or send it to AI Write to grow a one-liner into a longer scene or roleplay intro.

faq.name: faq
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Incorrect Quote Generator FAQ
faq.description: Short answers to the main scope and usage questions.

items.title: What makes an incorrect quote different from normal dialogue generation?
items.description: An incorrect quote is intentionally compressed and out-of-context. The goal is a fast punchline or character dynamic, not a natural full scene — closer to a meme caption with named speakers than a polished chapter.

items.title: Can I use original characters instead of fandom characters?
items.description: Yes. The tool only needs names and a setup prompt, so it works for OCs, roleplay parties, or mixed groups.

items.title: Can I keep the result non-romantic or cleaner for public posting?
items.description: Yes. The built-in safety toggles can avoid romance, avoid shipping implications, and keep the output clean.

items.title: What should I do if I want to expand a quote into a full scene?
items.description: Use Continue in AI Write. That sends the generated quote into the writing workspace so you can develop it into a larger draft.

items.title: Is the incorrect quote generator free to use?
items.description: Yes. You can generate, copy, and continue quotes without signing up. AI Write expansion is also available from the same workflow.

items.title: How many characters can I add to a single quote?
items.description: Between two and six named characters. If you leave the character fields blank, the generator will assign generic speakers based on your prompt.

items.title: What output languages does the tool support?
items.description: English, Chinese, German, Korean, Japanese, and Russian. Pick an output language in the form before you generate, and the quote will be written in that language.

cta.name: cta
cta.title: Spin Up Your First Misread Quote
cta.description: Run the incorrect quote generator now, keep the funny ones, and send the best lines to AI Write when you want to expand them into a larger draft.
